name: pymeta3-wheel

metadata.name: PyMeta3-wheel
metadata.summary: Pattern-matching language based on OMeta for Python 3 and 2

documentation:
# PyMeta3

## A Pattern-Matching Language Based on Python

This is a fork of PyMeta 0.5.0 that supports Python 2 and 3.

### Installation

pip install PyMeta3

### Summary

PyMeta is an implementation of OMeta, an object-oriented pattern-matching
language developed by Alessandro Warth
(<http://www.cs.ucla.edu/~awarth/ometa/>). PyMeta provides a compact syntax based
on Parsing Expression Grammars (PEGs) for common lexing, parsing and
tree-transforming activities in a way that's easy to reason about for Python
programmers.

### How It Works

PyMeta compiles a grammar to a Python class, with the rules as methods. The
rules specify parsing expressions, which consume input and return values if
they succeed in matching.

#### Basic syntax

`foo ::= ....`

: Define a rule named foo.

`expr1 expr2`

: Match expr1, and then match expr2 if it succeeds, returning the value of
  expr2. Like Python's `and`.

`expr1 | expr2`

: Try to match expr1 --- if it fails, match expr2 instead. Like Python's
  `or`.

`expr*`

: Match expr zero or more times, returning a list of matches.

`expr+`

: Match expr one or more times, returning a list of matches.

`expr?`

: Try to match expr. Returns None if it fails to match.

`~expr`

: Fail if the next item in the input matches expr.

`<ruleName>`

: Call the rule `ruleName`.

`'x'`

: Match the literal character 'x'.

`expr:name`

: Bind the result of expr to the local variable `name`.

`=> pythonExpression`

: Evaluate the given Python expression and return its result.

Comments like Python comments are supported as well, starting with #
and extending to the end of the line.

### Interface

The starting point for defining a new grammar is
`pymeta.grammar.OMeta.makeGrammar`, which takes a grammar definition and a
dict of variable bindings for its embedded expressions and produces a Python
class. Grammars can be subclassed as usual, and makeGrammar can be called on
these classes to override rules and provide new ones. To invoke a grammar rule,
call `grammarObject.apply()` with its name.

### Example Usage

```pycon
>>> from pymeta.grammar import OMeta
>>> exampleGrammar = """
ones ::= '1' '1' => 1
twos ::= '2' '2' => 2
stuff ::= (<ones> | <twos>)+
"""
>>> Example = OMeta.makeGrammar(exampleGrammar, {})
>>> g = Example("11221111")
>>> result, error = g.apply("stuff")
>>> result
[1, 2, 1, 1]
```

modules.name: pymeta.grammar
documentation:
Public interface to OMeta, as well as the grammars used to compile grammar
definitions.
-*- test-case-name: pymeta.test.test_pymeta -*-

classes.name: pymeta.grammar.OMeta

methods.name: pymeta.grammar.OMeta.makeGrammar

documentation: Base class for grammar definitions.

classes.name: pymeta.grammar.OMetaGrammarMixin

methods.name: pymeta.grammar.OMetaGrammarMixin.parseGrammar
pymeta.grammar.OMetaGrammarMixin.applicationArgs
documentation:
Collect rule arguments, a list of Python expressions separated by
spaces.

methods.name: pymeta.grammar.OMetaGrammarMixin.ruleValueExpr
documentation:
Find and generate code for a Python expression terminated by a close
paren/brace or end of line.

methods.name: pymeta.grammar.OMetaGrammarMixin.semanticActionExpr
documentation:
Find and generate code for a Python expression terminated by a
close-paren, whose return value is ignored.

methods.name: pymeta.grammar.OMetaGrammarMixin.semanticPredicateExpr
documentation:
Find and generate code for a Python expression terminated by a
close-paren, whose return value determines the success of the pattern
it's in.

methods.name: pymeta.grammar.OMetaGrammarMixin.eatWhitespace
documentation: Consume input until a non-whitespace character is reached.
Helpers for the base grammar for parsing grammar definitions.

modules.name: pymeta.builder
documentation: -*- test-case-name: pymeta.test.test_builder -*-

classes.name: pymeta.builder.TreeBuilder
documentation: Produce an abstract syntax tree of OMeta operations.

classes.name: pymeta.builder.PythonWriter

methods.name: pymeta.builder.PythonWriter._gensym
documentation: Produce a unique name for a variable in generated code.

methods.name: pymeta.builder.PythonWriter._newThunkFor
pymeta.builder.PythonWriter._expr
documentation:
Generate the code needed to execute the expression, and return the
variable name bound to its value.

methods.name: pymeta.builder.PythonWriter._writeFunction
pymeta.builder.PythonWriter.compilePythonExpr
documentation: Generate code for running embedded Python expressions.

methods.name: pymeta.builder.PythonWriter.generate_Apply
documentation: Create a call to self.apply(ruleName, *args).

methods.name: pymeta.builder.PythonWriter.generate_Exactly
documentation: Create a call to self.exactly(expr).

methods.name: pymeta.builder.PythonWriter.generate_Many
documentation: Create a call to self.many(lambda: expr).

methods.name: pymeta.builder.PythonWriter.generate_Many1
documentation: Create a call to self.many(lambda: expr).

methods.name: pymeta.builder.PythonWriter.generate_Optional
documentation: Try to parse an expr and continue if it fails.

methods.name: pymeta.builder.PythonWriter.generate_Or
documentation:
Create a call to
self._or([lambda: expr1, lambda: expr2, ... , lambda: exprN]).

methods.name: pymeta.builder.PythonWriter.generate_Not
documentation: Create a call to self._not(lambda: expr).

methods.name: pymeta.builder.PythonWriter.generate_Lookahead
documentation: Create a call to self.lookahead(lambda: expr).

methods.name: pymeta.builder.PythonWriter.generate_And
documentation: Generate code for each statement in order.

methods.name: pymeta.builder.PythonWriter.generate_Bind
documentation: Bind the value of 'expr' to a name in the _locals dict.

methods.name: pymeta.builder.PythonWriter.generate_Predicate
documentation: Generate a call to self.pred(lambda: expr).

methods.name: pymeta.builder.PythonWriter.generate_Action
documentation: Generate this embedded Python expression on its own line.

methods.name: pymeta.builder.PythonWriter.generate_Python
documentation: Generate this embedded Python expression on its own line.

methods.name: pymeta.builder.PythonWriter.generate_List
documentation: Generate a call to self.listpattern(lambda: expr).
Converts an OMeta syntax tree into Python source.

classes.name: pymeta.builder.GeneratedCodeLoader
documentation:
Object for use as a module's __loader__, to display generated
source.

modules.name: pymeta.runtime
documentation: Code needed to run a grammar after it has been compiled.
-*- test-case-name: pymeta.test.test_runtime -*-

functions.name: pymeta.runtime.expected
documentation:
Return an indication of expected input and the position where it was
expected and not encountered.

functions.name: pymeta.runtime.eof
documentation: Return an indication that the end of the input was reached.

functions.name: pymeta.runtime.joinErrors
documentation: Return the error from the branch that matched the most of the input.

classes.name: pymeta.runtime.ParseError

methods.name: pymeta.runtime.ParseError.formatError
documentation: Return a pretty string containing error info about string parsing failure.
?Redo from start

classes.name: pymeta.runtime.character

methods.name: pymeta.runtime.character.__iter__
documentation:
Prevent string patterns and list patterns from matching single
characters.
Type to allow distinguishing characters from strings.

classes.name: pymeta.runtime.unicodeCharacter

methods.name: pymeta.runtime.unicodeCharacter.__iter__
documentation:
Prevent string patterns and list patterns from matching single
characters.
Type to distinguish characters from Unicode strings.

documentation: @param iterable: Any iterable Python object.

Returns the memo record for the named rule.
@param name: A rule name.

documentation: The basic input mechanism used by OMeta grammars.

Returns the memo record for the named rule.
@param name: A rule name.

methods.name: pymeta.runtime.ArgInput.setMemo

classes.name: pymeta.runtime.LeftRecursion
documentation: Marker for left recursion in a grammar rule.

classes.name: pymeta.runtime.OMetaBase

methods.name: pymeta.runtime.OMetaBase.__init__
pymeta.runtime.OMetaBase.superApply
documentation:
Apply the named rule as defined on this object's superclass.

@param ruleName: A rule name.

methods.name: pymeta.runtime.OMetaBase.apply
documentation:
Apply the named rule, optionally with some arguments.

@param ruleName: A rule name.

methods.name: pymeta.runtime.OMetaBase._apply
pymeta.runtime.OMetaBase.rule_anything
documentation: Match a single item from the input of any kind.

methods.name: pymeta.runtime.OMetaBase.exactly
documentation:
Match a single item from the input equal to the given specimen.

@param wanted: What to match.

methods.name: pymeta.runtime.OMetaBase.many
pymeta.runtime.OMetaBase._or
pymeta.runtime.OMetaBase._not
documentation:
Call the given function. Raise ParseError iff it does not.

@param fn: A callable of no arguments.

methods.name: pymeta.runtime.OMetaBase.eatWhitespace
documentation: Consume input until a non-whitespace character is reached.

methods.name: pymeta.runtime.OMetaBase.pred
documentation:
Call the given function, raising ParseError if it returns false.

@param expr: A callable of no arguments.

methods.name: pymeta.runtime.OMetaBase.listpattern
pymeta.runtime.OMetaBase.end
documentation: Match the end of the stream.

methods.name: pymeta.runtime.OMetaBase.lookahead
pymeta.runtime.OMetaBase.token
documentation: Match and return the given string, consuming any preceding whitespace.

methods.name: pymeta.runtime.OMetaBase.letter
documentation: Match a single letter.

methods.name: pymeta.runtime.OMetaBase.letterOrDigit
documentation: Match a single alphanumeric character.

methods.name: pymeta.runtime.OMetaBase.digit
documentation: Match a single digit.

methods.name: pymeta.runtime.OMetaBase.pythonExpr
documentation:
Extract a Python expression from the input and return it.

@arg endChars: A set of characters delimiting the end of the expression.

Base class providing implementations of the fundamental OMeta
operations. Built-in rules are defined here.

modules.name: pymeta.boot
documentation:
The definition of PyMeta's language is itself a PyMeta grammar, but something
has to be able to read that. Most of the code in this module is generated from
that grammar (in future versions, it will hopefully all be generated).
-*- test-case-name: pymeta.test.test_grammar -*-

classes.name: pymeta.boot.BootOMetaGrammar

methods.name: pymeta.boot.BootOMetaGrammar.parseGrammar
pymeta.boot.BootOMetaGrammar.eatWhitespace
documentation: Consume input until a non-whitespace character is reached.

The bootstrap grammar, generated from L{pymeta.grammar.OMetaGrammar} via
L{pymeta.builder.PythonBuilder}.
